methods.description: This method describes the collection and preservation of deep-sea sediment samples containing Chilostomella ovoidea from central Sagami Bay, Japan, during two separate research cruises. The purpose is to prepare the samples for further analysis by isolating and preserving foraminiferal specimens.

steps.description: Deep-sea sediments containing Chilostomella ovoidea were collected from central Sagami Bay, Japan, during cruise KS13-T2 of R/V Shinsei-Maru in October 2013 and cruise KM16-01 of R/V Kaimei in April 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: collect deep-sea sediment samples from specified coordinates in Sagami Bay

steps.description: During cruise KS13-T2, sediment samples were collected with a multiple corer and sliced immediately upon recovery on-board into 1-cm sections, to a depth of 6 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: slice sediment samples into 1-cm sections up to 6 cm depth

steps.description: Sliced sections were preserved at 4°C in separate plastic bags without additional seawater.

In the laboratory, deepsea bottom water was added to the sediment to remove fine particles by decantation, and foraminiferal specimens were collected immediately from the remaining sediments under a binocular stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: remove fine particles from sediment using decantation and collect foraminiferal specimens

steps.description: Thirteen specimens of C. ovoidea were collected from the 4-5 cm depth section and were fixed overnight in 2.5% glutaraldehyde in filtered seawater and stored at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: fix and store foraminiferal specimens in glutaraldehyde solution

steps.description: During cruise KM16-01, sediment samples were collected with a push corer attached to the remotely operated vehicle KM-ROV and were immediately sliced onboard into 0.5-or 1-cm sections to a depth of 6 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: slice sediment samples into 0.5 or 1-cm sections up to 6 cm depth using push corer

steps.description: The sediment samples were fixed in 10% glutaraldehyde to a final concentration of 4.0% glutaraldehyde.
steps.searchable_summary: fix sediment samples in 4.0% glutaraldehyde solution

steps.description: In the laboratory, specimens with an aggregation of sediment particles around their apertures and/or with tests mostly filled with cytoplasm were isolated from the > 125-µm size fraction using a binocular stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate specific foraminiferal specimens using a stereomicroscope

steps.description: Three isolated specimens from the 1-2 cm depth sections were preserved in 2.5% glutaraldehyde in filtered seawater and stored at 4°C prior to further processing.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve isolated specimens in glutaraldehyde solution
